scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B) events — both PRs alter the `events` table: Alan adds `webhook_status`/`delivery_attempts`, and Sarah adds `delivery_state`/`processor_version`.

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C) Sarah — she only works Monday, Wednesday, Friday. Thursday March 13 is a non-working day for her, and the workspace also notes she’s off that Thursday.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B) SAML SSO — Josh told Acme Corp it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly said SAML was not in this phase and would add 3 weeks.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B) The new flow removed 4 steps including **“first milestone”**, which used to be the activation metric trigger, so **“completion” now means something different**. The 85% completion rate isn’t apples-to-apples with the old 60% because the finish line moved.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace says it was created for “load testing” but was “never actually used for load testing,” making it the staging environment that wasn’t used for its intended purpose.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text:
B) All three were in migration batch-7, run on February 25.  
The tickets look different, but each one explicitly notes `Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25`, which is the shared link.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: C — Only the webhook payload format remains open. The doc summary says cursor vs offset pagination has consensus (Sarah recommends cursor, Alan agrees), while the webhook payload is still debated.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) `src/billing/mapping.rs` — Alan calls it “the rosetta stone file,” and it maps between `plan_id`, `tier_id`, and Stripe’s `subscription` naming.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B) CSV only — Josh’s research says all 3 prospects confirmed CSV is sufficient; none specifically نیاز PDF, and Excel came from the sales rep, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The suite has **0 edge case tests** and **7 end-to-end tests**. The workspace also shows the suite is **82% unit** and **16% integration**, with **0% edge case tests**, which matches the customer bugs around boundary conditions and race/routing issues.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B) Long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth — Sarah’s synthesis was that you can keep the developer experience simple like API keys while still getting JWT benefits like rotation and revocation.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The gap is **no delivery confirmation mechanism**. Even if the endpoint returns 200, we have no way to know whether the customer actually processed the webhook, so failures could surface much later as reconciliation issues.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B) Takeshi at NovaTech in Tokyo — he was explicitly offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective and hasn’t been consulted yet.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C) About 2.75 hours per week. The workspace explicitly totals the four items at **~2.75 hours/week**: **1h hardcoded URLs + 45m retry logic + 1h generic errors**, plus the skipped test causing missed bugs.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text:
C) 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah's existing webhook signature module means the change is localized — about half a day

The workspace notes two explicit prospect asks in the last two weeks, and says Sarah already has a webhook signature module, so adding Ed25519 should be localized rather than a new system.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text:
C) Feature 5 — break-even happens at 5 features.

From the workspace math: without refactor, 5 features cost 25 days; with refactor, 3 weeks + 5 features at 2 days = 25 days. Net positive starts at feature 6.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The root cause is a spec ambiguity. The doc says “subscription start date,” but engineering implemented the billing period start date (Feb 1) while Acme expected the original signup date (Jan 15). This is a 1-line UI change, not a backend or data bug.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B) Annual billing requires proration, refund, and dunning changes that would conflict with the March 31 billing migration launch.

The workspace shows Billing Migration Phase 2 is already on a hard March 31 target with no buffer, and it’s blocked on reconciliation plus webhook format finalization. Adding annual billing would introduce more billing-system work into the same critical path.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B) 12 minutes detection; root cause was a process gap — no testing against production-scale data with historical records. The incident notes Sarah’s alert fired at 09:12 after the 09:00 start, and the “what went wrong” section says the script wasn’t tested against production-scale data with full historical records.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text:
C) 95% utilization at baseline; during typical 1.5-2x spikes the combined 143-190 req/sec exceeds the 100 limit, causing 429 errors

The workspace says combined normal peak is 95 req/sec (60 + 35), which is 95% of the 100 req/sec limit. It also says 1.5-2x traffic spikes would push usage to 143-190 req/sec, exceeding the limit and triggering 429s.
